trivia_bullets.text: Village Rites was introduced in Core Set 2021 (July 2020) and is not a reprint — M21 was its debut printing. It has since been reprinted in Kaldheim (2021), Strixhaven Mystical Archive (2021, in a special showcase treatment), Jumpstart 2022, Game Night: Free-for-All (2022), The Lost Caverns of Ixalan Commander (2023), Foundations Jumpstart (2024), Innistrad Remastered (2025, two variants), and a Secret Lair Drop (December 2025). [Scryfall prints search]

Village Rites is a one-mana black instant: sacrifice a creature, draw two cards. As of the EDHREC dataset, it appears in over 221,000 Commander decks (EDHREC rank 194), making it one of the highest-penetration black staples in the format at common rarity. It is also legal in Pauper and Pauper Commander. [Scryfall legalities; EDHREC article 'Singleton Shmingleton — Village Rites']

EDHREC's 'Singleton Shmingleton' column described Village Rites as having 'opened the floodgates' on this effect type, tracing a lineage from older sacrifice-draw cards (Skulltap, Altar's Reap) to the modern tight-costed versions. The article noted it is the second-most-played card in its functional cycle, behind Deadly Dispute (232,316 decks vs. Village Rites' 221,385 at the time of writing). [EDHREC: 'Singleton Shmingleton — Village Rites']

Flavor text: "You were so eager to take and consume. Yet when asked to give, you refuse?" — speaker unnamed [Scryfall]. The rhetorical accusation directed at a sacrifice victim is consistent with cult ritual framing; no specific plane or faction is named on the card itself.

related_cards.name: Deadly Dispute
related_cards.note: Near-functional analog; also one mana, sacrifice, draw two plus a Treasure — slightly more upside, also a format staple

related_cards.name: Altar's Reap
related_cards.note: Earlier sacrifice-draw predecessor at two mana; Village Rites is the tighter descendant

related_cards.name: Viscera Seer
related_cards.note: Free sacrifice outlet that pairs with Village Rites in aristocrats strategies
